meta.subtitle: Plain-English market read · Gap-risk check · First session after the long Juneteenth weekend
meta.rerun_note: Core PCE inflation report Thu 6/25 8:30 AM · re-run after

verdict.label: YELLOW · Trade small or sit out
verdict.desc: Calmer open after the long weekend: U.S.–Iran talks showed "major progress" with a 60-day roadmap to a final deal, oil is sliding toward $74 and stocks are mixed (Dow up, Nasdaq soft). But the market is still digesting last week's hawkish Fed under new Chair Warsh — traders now price a possible rate HIKE later in 2026 and short-end yields stay firm, and Thursday's Core PCE inflation report is the week's big test. Take one small, well-supported trade at most, or wait for the inflation read.

composite.band_note: MILD · down from 48 · weekend passed cleanly, PCE still ahead

gap.band_note: Below the danger line · the bigger risk is Thursday's PCE inflation report

focus.jbig: Quiet open · digesting a hawkish Fed
focus.jtag: Oil falls on Iran progress; PCE inflation lands Thursday
focus.jnote: Today is a calmer "wait for the data" day. The weekend passed without a shock: Iran reported "major progress" and both sides agreed a roadmap to a final deal within 60 days, which pushed oil toward $74 — near its lowest since early March. But last week's hawkish Fed still hangs over everything — nine of 18 officials penciled in at least one 2026 hike, and the 10-year yield sits near 4.45% as traders bet on a possible hike. Underneath, money is rotating: small caps (Russell 2000 +2.1%) and chips lead while AI hyperscalers (Alphabet, Amazon, Meta) lag. The cleanest fresh catalyst is the AbbVie–Apogee $10.9B all-cash buyout. Thursday's Core PCE inflation report is the real test, so size down.
focus.notdown: Industrials and financials held the Dow firm, and small caps (Russell 2000) bounced hard (+2.1%) as yields eased slightly — but that's rotation, not a single named catalyst, and a hawkish Fed could reverse it. Low grade.
focus.notup: Energy (XLE, XOM, CVX, OXY) is the cleaner bearish read: oil is sliding toward $74 on the Iran 60-day roadmap, Hormuz reopening, and Gulf producers ramping output. Real, durable news — but it's pre-PCE, so half size at most.
focus.why: Why YELLOW and not GREEN? The gap-risk numbers (41 and 22%) are calm enough for a green light — but two rules apply: respect a fresh policy shock (the Fed just flipped from cuts to possible hikes) and don't go full size right before a major inflation print. Core PCE lands Thursday 8:30 AM. Take one small, well-supported trade if it checks out at 9:35 AM, or wait. A cleaner read returns after PCE.

how_built.name: Volatility — VIX ~16.4–16.8, calm, near its long-term average
Big events (next 1–3 days) — Core PCE inflation report Thursday + flash PMIs Tuesday
World risk — Iran 60-day roadmap calming things, but no signed final deal yet
Volatility trend — easing back down after last week's Fed spike
Commodities — oil down ~1–2%, which is calming for inflation, not scary

setups.read: Best idea. Oil is sliding toward $74 on the Iran 60-day roadmap, Hormuz reopening, and Gulf producers ramping output — that caps the whole group. Real, durable news, but it's pre-PCE, so trade half size.
AbbVie is buying it for cash (~$10.9B) — the stock is pinned near the deal price. Once a name agrees to an all-cash buyout it stops moving, so there's no spread edge here. Skip.

Chips extended their historic rally (Micron, Sandisk +5%), but that's momentum, not a single clean catalyst — and higher-for-longer rates pressure them. Micron reports later this week, adding earnings risk. Wait.
Today's leaders with the Dow firm and banks (BofA, JPM) up ~2%, but it's broad rotation with no single news story and a hawkish Fed could reverse it. Wait.
Cutting ~18% of U.S. staff and losing its COO is real news, but it's a low-priced, thin-option stock down only ~2% with little support to lean on. Not a clean Sniper setup.

scenarios.label: Most likely — Quiet, choppy digestion ahead of PCE
scenarios.sub: Money keeps rotating; Dow firm, tech mixed; everyone waits for Thursday's inflation read

scenarios.label: Bad case — Yields climb, tech rolls over
scenarios.sub: Hike fears stick and the short end stays firm → pressure on long-duration growth and AI hyperscalers

scenarios.label: Good case — Soft PCE hopes, oil keeps falling
scenarios.sub: Cheaper oil eases the inflation story and dip-buyers step in → broad relief rally

scenarios.label: Surprise — Iran talks stumble or a shock hits
scenarios.sub: The 60-day roadmap breaks down or a hot data point lands → oil and volatility jump

risks: Core PCE inflation · Thu 6/25 8:30 AM — the Fed's favorite inflation gauge; PPI components that map to PCE suggest a firm print, so a hot number could push yields and hike odds higher.
Hike fears rising · short end firm — markets now price a possible Fed rate hike later in 2026; growth and tech can drop fast if yields keep climbing.
Iran deal not signed yet — it's a 60-day roadmap built on last week's MOU; oil could whipsaw on any setback.
Micron (MU) earnings this week — don't sell spreads on that name into its report; the print adds gap risk.
S&P Global flash PMIs · Tuesday 6/23 — a soft reading could shift the growth-vs-inflation debate quickly.

rows.today: Iran 60-day roadmap + AbbVie–Apogee M&A; PCE Thu
